phases.name: Fix Database Connection Tests
phases.description: Fix 9 failing tests in services/job-service/repository/job_assignment_test.go. All tests fail with 'dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5432: connect: connection refused'. The test suite requires PostgreSQL to be running but it's not available during test execution. Options: 1) Add testcontainers/go for automated PostgreSQL container lifecycle in tests, 2) Add docker-compose.test.yml for test infrastructure, 3) Mock the database layer for unit tests. Implement testcontainers approach as it provides real database testing. Create internal/testutil/db.go with testcontainers setup. Update job_assignment_test.go to use the test database helper. Ensure tests cleanup properly after each test run.
success_criteria: testcontainers setup works on any developer machine
tests are isolated and don't interfere with each other

phases.name: Add Test Environment Setup
phases.description: Create comprehensive test infrastructure for all repository tests. Create internal/testutil package with database helpers, Redis helpers using testcontainers, and mock S3 storage for storage-service tests. Add Makefile targets: 'make test-db' starts PostgreSQL and Redis, 'make test-all' runs all tests with dependencies, 'make test-integration' runs only integration tests. Add scripts/test-env.sh for quick test environment bootstrap. Document test setup in CONTRIBUTING.md. This ensures any developer can run full test suite without manual infrastructure setup.
success_criteria: make test-all runs all tests without manual setup
Redis-dependent tests also pass with testcontainers

phases.name: Implement CI/CD Pipeline
phases.description: Create GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/ci.yml for continuous integration. Pipeline should: 1) Run go test ./... with testcontainers for database services, 2) Run cd web/dashboard && npm run type-check && npm test, 3) Build all services with go build ./..., 4) Run docker-compose build to verify Dockerfiles, 5) Run security scanning with golangci-lint and npm audit. Add status badges to README.md. Create .github/workflows/release.yml for automated releases on tags. This ensures code quality checks run automatically on every pull request.
success_criteria: CI workflow runs successfully on push to main/PR branches
All checks (tests, type-check, lint, build) pass in CI

phases.name: Add Service Inter-Communication Tests
phases.description: Create tests/integration/service_communication_test.go to test inter-service communication. Test auth-service issuing JWT tokens that other services accept. Test job-service calling registry-service for agent discovery. Test notification-service WebSocket connections receiving job status updates. Use testcontainers to spin up all services together. Verify that: 1) Auth tokens propagate correctly between services, 2) Service mesh routing works via gateway, 3) Pub/sub via Redis for async events functions properly. These tests catch integration issues before production.

phases.name: Add Observability Dashboard
phases.description: Implement observability features for production monitoring. Add Prometheus metrics endpoint /metrics to all services using prometheus/client_golang. Track: request count, latency, error rate by endpoint. Add structured logging with zap, with trace ID correlation. Create Grafana dashboard configurations in deployments/grafana/dashboards/ for: service health, request metrics, database connection pool, Redis queue depth. Update docker-compose.yml to include Prometheus and Grafana containers. Document metrics in docs/observability.md. This enables production monitoring and debugging.

phases.name: Implement Rate Limiting
phases.description: Add rate limiting middleware to protect against abuse. Create internal/shared/middleware/ratelimit.go using Redis-backed token bucket algorithm. Apply limits: 100 req/min for authenticated users, 10 req/min for unauthenticated. Add special handling for expensive endpoints (document upload, bulk job creation). Configure limits via environment variables: RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED, RATE_LIMIT_AUTHENTICATED, RATE_LIMIT_ANONYMOUS. Add X-RateLimit-* headers to responses. Update tests to verify rate limiting works. This prevents DoS and resource exhaustion.
success_criteria: Rate limiting active on all public endpoints

phases.name: Add Kubernetes Deployment Manifests
phases.description: Create Kubernetes deployment manifests for production deployment. Create deployments/k8s/ directory with: namespace.yaml, configmap.yaml for environment config, secret.yaml for sensitive data, PostgreSQL StatefulSet with persistent volume, Redis deployment, each service's Deployment and Service, gateway Ingress with TLS, HorizontalPodAutoscaler for each service. Add Helm chart in deployments/helm/ for easier installation. Document deployment process in docs/deployment.md. This enables cloud-native deployment on any Kubernetes cluster.
success_criteria: kubectl apply -f deployments/k8s/ deploys all resources

critical_issues: 9/9 job-service repository tests fail due to PostgreSQL unavailable
No CI/CD pipeline for automated testing
No test infrastructure automation for database/Redis dependencies
strengths: Complete frontend UI components with no TODOs
Comprehensive service architecture (auth, registry, job, storage, notification, gateway, analytics, org)

rationale: Phase 1 addresses the immediate blocker: 9 failing tests due to missing PostgreSQL. Using testcontainers ensures tests work anywhere. Phase 2 extends this to all services for consistent test infrastructure. Phase 3 adds CI/CD to prevent future regressions—critical for team productivity. Phase 4 validates inter-service communication which is a common failure point in microservices. Phase 5 adds observability needed for production operations. Phase 6 adds security hardening with rate limiting. Phase 7 enables production-scale Kubernetes deployment. This ordering follows the rules: fix tests first, then add automation (CI), then validate integration, then production hardening and deployment.
